matter.title: Riverbend Analytics LLC v. John G. Inglewood et al. (short-term rental)
matter.summary: Riverbend Analytics LLC sues unit owner John G. Inglewood and co-owner/manager John D. Kingsley to enforce an hoa rule prohibiting transient/short-term rentals against a short-term rental at an oceanfront condominium listed on Airbnb, to abate a private nuisance, and for declaratory and injunctive relief; Town of Harborwick is joined because zoning does not list short-term rentals as a permitted use.

defendant_2.role: Co-owner / property manager of the STR

fact_pattern.summary: Riverbend Analytics LLC sues unit owner John G. Inglewood and co-owner/manager John D. Kingsley to enforce an hoa rule prohibiting transient/short-term rentals against a short-term rental at an oceanfront condominium listed on Airbnb, to abate a private nuisance, and for declaratory and injunctive relief; Town of Harborwick is joined because zoning does not list short-term rentals as a permitted use.
fact_pattern.narrative:
John G. Inglewood and John D. Kingsley own and operate a short-term rental at an oceanfront condominium governed by Riverbend Analytics LLC. Beginning on or about 2023-11-27, they listed the unit on Airbnb for transient stays at roughly $1379 per night, generating on the order of $146799 in annual rental revenue.

The association points to an hoa rule prohibiting transient/short-term rentals, which it says the short-term-rental use violates. Neighboring owners have complained of recurring disturbances from the rotating guests, which the association characterizes as a private nuisance. The association sent the operators a cease-and-desist demand, but the rental has continued.

On the land-use side, zoning does not list short-term rentals as a permitted use, and Town of Harborwick is joined as a party so the court can sort out the interplay of the recorded restriction, the town's short-term-rental ordinance, and zoning. The association filed this action in Sagadahoc County Superior Court seeking to enforce the covenant, abate the nuisance, and obtain declaratory and injunctive relief stopping or strictly limiting the rental, plus its enforcement costs and attorney fees.
undisputed_facts: John G. Inglewood owns a unit governed by Riverbend Analytics LLC and has offered it for short-term rental on Airbnb since on or about 2023-11-27.
Riverbend Analytics LLC's governing documents include an hoa rule prohibiting transient/short-term rentals.
The association sent a cease-and-desist demand and Town of Harborwick has been joined as a party to the action.
disputed_facts: Whether the short-term-rental use violates the recorded restriction, or whether it is a permitted residential use of the property.
Whether the operation is a private nuisance and, if so, the appropriate abatement.
Whether the town's short-term-rental ordinance and zoning permit or bar the rental, and how they interact with the recorded covenant.
Whether the association has selectively or inconsistently enforced the restriction against this owner.

timeline.event: John and John began listing the unit for short-term rental on Airbnb.
Association's complaint filed in Bath Superior Court to enforce the covenant, abate the nuisance, and obtain injunctive relief.

third_parties.name: Town Code Enforcement Office
third_parties.relevance: Administers the town's STR ordinance and zoning and holds the registration, permit, and violation records.

third_parties.name: Maria T. Coe
third_parties.relevance: Lives beside the rental and logged repeated complaints of noise, trash, and parking from the short-term guests.

third_parties.name: Riverbend Analytics LLC
third_parties.role: Property management / cleaning company servicing the STR
third_parties.relevance: Manages bookings and turnovers for the rental and can speak to occupancy, frequency, and guest conduct.

third_parties.name: Airbnb, Inc.
third_parties.relevance: Hosts the listing and holds the booking, payout, and review records for the short-term rental.

third_parties.name: Harborwick Police Department
third_parties.role: Local police on the noise calls
third_parties.relevance: Responded to noise and disturbance calls at the rental and holds the call and incident records.

exchanges.question: Who am I speaking with on behalf of the association?
exchanges.answer: I am the board president of the association for an oceanfront condominium. The board voted to authorize this action against the owner who keeps short-term renting the unit.

exchanges.question: What do your governing documents say about rentals?
exchanges.answer: We have an hoa rule prohibiting transient/short-term rentals. We read that as barring exactly this kind of nightly, transient rental, but the owner insists it is just a permitted residential use.

exchanges.question: What has the short-term rental actually done to the community?
exchanges.answer: Since about 2023-11-27 they have been booking the unit on Airbnb for around $1379 a night. We have had a string of disturbances from the constant turnover of strangers, and the other owners are fed up.

exchanges.question: What have you already tried?
exchanges.answer: We sent a cease-and-desist letter and asked them to stop, but the listing is still up. The town is part of the picture too, because zoning does not list short-term rentals as a permitted use.

exchanges.question: How does the town's ordinance affect this?
exchanges.answer: It is tangled. zoning does not list short-term rentals as a permitted use, so we joined the town to get a clear ruling on how the ordinance and zoning line up with our covenant.

exchanges.question: What does a good outcome look like?
exchanges.answer: Stop or strictly limit the short-term rental, end the disturbances, get clear guidance under both our covenant and the town's rules, and recover our enforcement costs and attorney fees.

goals: Stop or strictly limit the short-term-rental use of the unit through enforcement of the recorded restriction.
Abate the private nuisance caused by the transient rental's noise, traffic, trash, and turnover.
Recover the association's enforcement costs and reasonable attorney fees.
Obtain a declaratory judgment clarifying the parties' rights under the covenant and the town's short-term-rental ordinance and zoning.
priorities: Establishing that the short-term rental violates the recorded restriction.
Protecting the residential character and quiet enjoyment of the community.
Resolving the interplay of the covenant, the town ordinance, and zoning in one action.
desired_outcomes: An injunction stopping or strictly limiting the short-term rental of the unit.
A declaratory judgment and an award of the association's enforcement costs and attorney fees.
non_starters: Any resolution that lets the unrestricted short-term rental continue to the detriment of the other owners.

constraints.budget: Adequate through trial, but the board prefers an enforceable settlement that stops the rental.
constraints.timeline: Wants the rental enjoined or curtailed before the next peak booking season.

issues.issue: Entitlement to injunctive relief and attorney fees
sub_questions: Are the requirements for a permanent injunction satisfied?
Are the association's reasonable attorney fees recoverable?
issues.client_position: The association is entitled to an injunction against the rental and to recover its reasonable attorney fees.

issues.issue: Whether the short-term rental violates the recorded restrictive covenant limiting use to residential purposes
issues.governing_law: Recorded declaration; common law of restrictive covenants
sub_questions: Does a 'residential use' covenant bar transient, nightly rentals?
Is the restriction clear enough to be enforced against the operator?
issues.client_position: The covenant bars short-term rentals, and the nightly transient use violates the residential-use restriction.

issues.issue: Whether the association selectively or inconsistently enforced the restriction
issues.governing_law: Common law; doctrines of waiver and acquiescence
sub_questions: Has the association permitted other short-term rentals?
Has any waiver or acquiescence undermined enforcement here?
issues.client_position: The association has enforced the restriction consistently and has not waived its right to enforce it.

issues.issue: Whether the short-term-rental operation is an actionable private nuisance
sub_questions: Do the noise, parking, trash, and turnover unreasonably interfere with the other owners' use and enjoyment?
issues.client_position: The transient rental's recurring disturbances are an actionable private nuisance warranting abatement.

issues.issue: Whether the town's short-term-rental ordinance and zoning permit or bar the rental
sub_questions: Is short-term transient rental a permitted use under the zoning ordinance?
Does the town's STR registration or cap ordinance apply to this unit?
issues.client_position: The town's ordinance and zoning do not authorize this unpermitted transient use of the unit.

issues.issue: The association's power to enforce the declaration and to lien for assessments and fines
sub_questions: May the association enforce the use restriction on behalf of the owners?
May it levy and lien fines and assessments for the violation?
issues.client_position: The Condominium Act empowers the association to enforce the restriction and to lien for fines and assessments.

expert_opinions.topic: Noise levels generated by the short-term-rental guests
expert_opinions.opinion: Measured guest-generated noise exceeds the levels reasonably expected in the residential community.
expert_opinions.basis: On-site sound-level monitoring and analysis of the complaint and call records.

evidence.title: Photographs and video of the disturbances
evidence.description: Photographs and video documenting noise, parking, trash, and occupancy problems from the short-term guests.

evidence.title: Screenshots of the Airbnb/VRBO listings and reviews
evidence.description: Captures of the unit's short-term-rental listings, nightly rates, calendar, and guest reviews on Airbnb.

evidence.title: Cease-and-desist letters
evidence.description: The association's cease-and-desist demands to the operators and any responses.

evidence.title: Recorded declaration, covenants, and HOA rules
evidence.description: The recorded declaration and the association's rules containing the use restriction and the no-short-term-rental provision.

evidence.title: Town short-term-rental ordinance and permit decision
evidence.description: The text of the town's STR ordinance and zoning provisions and any registration or permit decision for the unit.

evidence.title: Neighbor complaint logs and police call records
evidence.description: The association's complaint logs and the police call and incident records for the rental.

evidence.title: The short-term rental's revenue records
evidence.description: Booking and payout records showing the frequency of stays and the revenue from the rental.

amounts.label: Association's enforcement and attorney-fee costs
amounts.basis: Attorney fees and costs of enforcing the restriction and abating the nuisance.

amounts.label: Cost of the appraisal and planning experts
amounts.basis: Retained appraisal and land-use planning experts.

amounts.label: Fines accrued under the town ordinance
amounts.basis: Per-violation fines under the town's short-term-rental ordinance.

amounts.label: Claimed diminution in neighboring property values
amounts.basis: Appraiser's estimate of value loss to the affected units.

causes_of_action.claim: Enforcement of the restrictive covenant barring short-term rentals
causes_of_action.against: John G. Inglewood and John D. Kingsley
elements: A valid recorded restriction limiting use to residential purposes
The owners' short-term-rental use of the unit
That the transient use violates the restriction
The association's right to enforce it
causes_of_action.relief_sought: An order enforcing the covenant and prohibiting the short-term-rental use

elements: The other owners' interest in the use and enjoyment of their property
An unreasonable and substantial interference from the transient rental
causes_of_action.relief_sought: Abatement of the nuisance and damages

causes_of_action.claim: Declaratory and injunctive relief to stop the short-term-rental use
elements: An actual controversy over the rental's legality under the covenant and ordinance
A clear legal right warranting injunctive relief
A balance of equities favoring the association
causes_of_action.relief_sought: A declaration of the parties' rights and an injunction stopping or strictly limiting the rental

causes_of_action.claim: Enforcement of the association's assessment and fine lien
elements: A perfected lien for assessments and fines
Fines and assessments levied for the violation and unpaid
The foreclosure is within the enforcement period
causes_of_action.relief_sought: Judgment enforcing the lien for the unpaid fines and assessments, interest, and reasonable attorney fees

affirmative_defenses.basis: Any disturbances are isolated and do not rise to a substantial and unreasonable interference constituting a private nuisance.
The association has permitted other short-term rentals and has waived or acquiesced in the practice it now seeks to enforce.

affirmative_defenses.defense: Covenant does not clearly bar short-term rentals
affirmative_defenses.basis: The recorded restriction does not unambiguously prohibit nightly or short-term rentals and must be construed in favor of the free use of property.
Renting a residence to occupants is a residential use of the property and does not violate a residential-use covenant.

counterclaims.claim: Declaratory judgment that short-term rentals are permitted
counterclaims.relief: A declaration that the recorded covenant and the town's ordinance permit the owners' short-term rental of the unit.

motions.title: Motion to consolidate with the Rule 80B permit appeal
motions.relief: Consolidation of the related 80B permit appeal with this enforcement action.

motions.title: Motion to compel platform booking records
motions.relief: An order compelling production of the booking and payout records from the operators and the platform.

motions.title: Motion for summary judgment on the covenant's meaning
motions.relief: A ruling that the recorded restriction bars short-term rentals as a matter of law.

motions.title: Motion for a preliminary injunction
motions.relief: An order enjoining the short-term rental of the unit while the action is pending.

docket.entry: Complaint filed to enforce the covenant, abate the nuisance, and for declaratory and injunctive relief.
Motion for a preliminary injunction filed and set for hearing.
Defendants' answer and counterclaim for declaratory judgment filed.
Town's answer filed and order on the preliminary-injunction motion entered.
Scheduling order entered; the related 80B permit appeal consolidated.
Order on the motion for summary judgment on the covenant's meaning entered.

facts.declaration_restriction: an HOA rule prohibiting transient/short-term rentals
nuisance_complaints: strangers and turnover disrupting the community
facts.town_str_ordinance: zoning does not list short-term rentals as a permitted use
